DURING THE GAME:
Click at the top of the screen or press ESC to use the menus. Additional shortcuts are shown there.
Click Right or Shift-Click Left Mouse
Button on an object to look at it.

     IN TYPING WINDOWS:
Arrows, Home and End move the cursor, or click anywhere with the mouse. Ctrl-C clears the line.

     IN DIALOG WINDOWS:
Select the outlined item with <Enter> and
use Tab and Shift-Tab to move between 
choices. 
Or click with the mouse to select an item.

ESC always cancels.

You can change your mind
   if you have one.
Every hero needs a break now and then.  Have a nice nap.
Sorry, there's not enough room to see that here.
Ten silver coins equal a gold coin in value.
Each gold coin is worth 10 silver coins.
Daggers for throwing and close-in fighting.
Armor made of leather boiled in wax.
Small apples from last Fall's harvest.
This gem glows with a magic of its own.
The gaudily-ornamented music box plays "Fur Elise".
Tool kit with several types of lockpicks.
An empty flask with a cork stopper.
A flask of genuine Fairy Dust.
Crystal clear water in a glass flask.
Troll beard with hairs as tough as wire.
A potion to restore Health Points.
A potion to restore Magic Points.
A potion to restore Stamina Points.
An oil to protect you from the Living Dead.
A mirror with a face (not yours) on it.
You drink a flask of water.  It tastes great.
Perhaps you could tell me which potion you want to drink.
It's not clear how you want to use that.
You don't have any of those.
The drink soothes as it goes down.
The drink burns as it goes down.
You don't feel anything.  Perhaps this was not the correct way to use this potion.
You feel a tingling sensation as you rub the unguent all over your body.
You can't.  You don't have any.
Didn't anyone warn you about eating other people's food?  Death by Toadstools doesn't leave mushroom for improvement.
Where did all the pretty horses go?
The mushrooms from the Fairy Ring are pretty good, so you hear.  However, when they're mixed with Kobold toadstools...that's a horse of a different color.
You eat some of the Kobold's fungus.  Within seconds your insides feel like they're on fire.  Guess you're not a Kobold, huh?
You think that you'd better take it easy for a while until you recover.
You ingest a few of the mushrooms from the Faery Ring.  They taste delicious.
Wow!  Look at all the nice paisley horses!
Not to mention the beautiful neon sky.
That's funny.  I could have sworn I warned you about eating too many Magic Mushrooms.  Your mind permanently warped, you die a garishly polka-dotted death.
Hmm, that was interesting.  But it would probably not be a good idea to repeat the experience.
You eat the Magic Acorn.  It tastes terrible.  Better stick to real food next time.
There's no need.  You're not hungry.
You eat some of the apples.  They actually taste quite good.

You eat some vegetables.  You think they would have been better saved for cows or horses.
Real Heroes eat preserved dry rations.
The rations are tasteless but filling.
Ugh.  You don't want to eat *that*.
You just can't sleep during the daytime.
You awake as the sun begins to rise.

While you were asleep, something decided to make a meal of you.  You're not sure what it was, but you don't really care at this point.  You shouldn't go to sleep where the creatures of the night can get you.
You're too impatient to rest right now.
After %d minutes rest, you feel better.
You do not have any daggers.
You do not have any rocks.
You pick up a few small rocks.
Suddenly a feeling of peace and tranquility permeates the area.
C'mon now! That's HARDLY a heroic action!
I'm sorry, but this game has a PG rating.
You didn't really say that, did you?
I'm not programmed to do that sort of thing!
The pressure gets to you after a while, doesn't it?
Didn't your Momma teach you any better than that?
It is just as it appears.
"Yes, the weather does what the weather will do, does it not?"
"I'm not sure that I understand you"
"You seem to be talking without saying very much."
"Welcome back! It is good to see you again. So many adventurers never return. Someday, you and I will go on an adventure together."
"Hrrmph! Ahem! Yes, as I was saying....where were we?"
"Oh, what a glorious day it was! I...... Oh, hello there!"
"You fiendish witch! I will have you...... Oh, it's you, my friend!"
"Oh, Barnard, bravest of the brave, you cannot be gone from us so soon.... What? What? You're still here, my fellow adventurer?"

We're sorry, but the pixel you clicked on is not in service at this time.  Please check your mouse and click again.
Thank you!
Of all the places to click on, you had to choose THIS one!
If there was something interesting and informative at this location on the screen, we would certainly have put up a much more interesting and informative message than this one.

This is a pixel.

If this had been a REAL picture element, you would have seen a REAL message.
You have reached the residence of Mr. and Mrs. Pixel.  We are unable to come to the screen right now, but if you leave your game and character at the sound of the beep...

There is a place between shadow and substance...
between reality and imagination...
but THIS isn't it!
Yes!  You have found a cleverly hidden representation of the Starship You-Know Who!

You may have already won a fabulous, all-expense-paid trip for two to scenic Shapeir, land of the shifting sands.  To claim your prize, simply purchase a soon-to-be-released copy of "Quest for Glory II:  Trial by Fire" at a software dealer near you.
Congratulations!  By clicking at this spot on the screen, you have initiated an irreversible process that will destroy all life as we know it.  Have a nice day!
It is the middle of the night
You are carrying so much that you can hardly move.  You'd better drop something soon.

Suddenly, you hear voices, seeming to come from everywhere at once.  They all sound like Baba Yaga, and they all say:
"Wheeeeeere's myyyyyy
Maaaandraaaaake Rooooooooooooot????"
Because you failed to meet Baba Yaga's DEADline, her curse turns you into a frog on the spot, and you are forced to live out your years dodging Sauruses (Saurii?) with large feet.
The tingling sensation is wearing off.
Suddenly, the deadly poison Fragmentation Bug leaps out of a crack in the system, and injects you with its poison.  Alas, there is no cure, save to . . .
You don't have time -- there's the slight matter of the monster that wants to eat you.
You pick up the loose daggers.
You retrieve your knives from the dead monster's body, and carefully wipe them clean for reuse.
You sense no magic in this area.
Your weapon is now magically charged.
You don't have a weapon to charge.
I don't know what you're trying to drop.
You can't drop something you don't have.
You're about as stealthy as the average Goon.
There's no point in trying that here.
You delicately insert the lockpick into your left nostril.
Unfortunately, you push it in too far, causing yourself a cerebral hemorrhage.  Guess you should have practiced some more on less difficult locks.
Success!  You now have an open nose.
You don't have an appropriate tool.
You're not close enough to a door.
You open the music box, which plays Beethoven's "Fur Elise."  After listening to it for a bit, you close the box.
You don't have a box to open.
You have no reason to do that.

You are exhausted from lack of sleep.
You are so exhausted that everything you do hurts.  Better get some rest.
That was a little too much for you.  You collapse from exhaustion and die.

The great house of Baron Stefan von Spielburg rises on a low hillock over the courtyard and castle walls.
There are guard towers at regular intervals around the castle wall.
The gatehouse is a massive structure with a portcullis closing off access to the castle.
It's a gate that raises and lowers.
The castle walls are thick and massive.  There is a walkway around the rampart for the guards to patrol.
The main courtyard is a broad, grassy area with a large octagonal area of flagstone paving in the center.
The flagstones are from a rock quarry in eastern Germany.
Well...maybe the flagstones are from western Germany.
Granite from our very own moutains?
Quit sightseeing and watch what you're doing!

C'mon!  He's not going to KILL you...
is he?
Why would you want to do that?
"Pity.  I'm in the mood for some good combat practice."
"I am a skilled teacher as well as a skilled fighter."
"However, the combat style I teach relies on the subtle alternation of Strike and Parry.  As I observe that you have no skill in the use of Parry, you would receive no benefit from my lessons."
"For a mere gold coin, I will be glad to give you some practice and some pointers on how to better your skills."
You pay the Weapon Master's price and then...
That's easy to say, but you don't HAVE a gold coin.  You make your apologies to the Weapon Master.
"I'm sorry.  Since you have no skill in parrying blows, you would receive no benefit from my instruction."
The man has the muscles of a trained athlete and is apparently quite skilled with the sword.
The weapon master carries a blunted practice sword, in the belief that students retain more of their learning if they survive the practice sessions.
"Please don't bore me, young adventurer.  Ask me about something interesting to me."
"Fighting is both an art and a skill."
"It is a skill in the use of the muscle strength and agility's control."
"It is an art in the out-witting and out-maneuvering of the opponent."
"I am, of course, a most skilled artist."
"If you were a swordsman skilled in both attack and parry, we could have had a practice bout."
"The sword is the finest of all weapons.  It requires an equal measure of skill and strength."
"You will find them very skilled at fighting.  After all, I am the one who trains all of the guards at this castle."
"The Baron had the wisdom to recognize greatness when I presented myself.  Thus he made me Weapon Master."
"The Baronet had some minor talent with weapons, but he was too impatient.  If he had but listened and learned what I teach, he would never have been lost."
"I never use a shield or armor under most circumstances.  It only gets in the way of my agility and dodging ability.  It is best used by those who are strong, but not swift or agile."
"Practice is the way to refine and improve one's skills."
"If you are addressing me, sir, you must respect me enough to address me to my face!"
"I can see now that my career must end.  Anytime that I, the Weapon Master, can be beaten by a mindless, nasty little twerp such as you, it is time to take an interminable vacation."
"By your leave, I hope your sword rusts and your cape fragments in the most awful manner!"
The lesson wears you down, and you become exhausted.
You are forced to admit that the Weapon Master has gotten the better of you.
"Even as we fought, I could detect your skills improving through practice."
"It was most prudent that you should give in to my superior skill."
"Still, you seem to tire more easily than you should."
"Be more aware of the angle with which your weapon approaches your opponent.  The thrust bites quick and deep, but the swing will do more damage if it connects."
"The art of dancing would be a good skill for you to practice, my friend.  The balance of the body over the rapidly moving feet has won many a contest."
"You have a tendency to overbalance when you dodge a blow.  This will adversely affect the timing and effectiveness of your counterattack."
"Put your attention to the control of your legs when you duck a swing.  Your springiness makes you seem to bounce, and a good opponent (like myself) will always take advantage of the opening."

"If you would like a stretcher, my weak, tired friend...
You seem a shadow of the person who began this little lesson."
"Should we meet again, I would not be adverse to another go-round.  Farewell, friend!"
The castle is northeast of here
The building's appearance is deceptive; it evidently extends well into the castle wall.  From your present vantage point, the barracks looks like it could house some sixty guards.
You see the castle and the guards.
You see the main courtyard of the castle and the stables beyond.
Along the wall to the southeast is the gatehouse.  A line of bushes partially obscure a defensive pit along the wall.
You can see the barracks and a man sitting in front of them.
You barely get to sleep when the guard on night patrol kicks you out.
The guard looks like he is either very tired or very lazy.
You're not close enough.  He's hard of hearing.
"If you want to fight, go talk to the Weapons Master."

As you approach the guard with your weapon, he jumps up and opens the barracks door as he yells for help.  Four guards playing cards inside the barracks quickly grab their weapons and overwhelm you.  As you are dragged into the castle and down into the dungeon, you realize that was probably not quite the right thing to do.
"I'm not here to answer your stupid questions.  Go talk to Karl the gatekeeper.  He talks to anyone about almost anything."
These are the mountains that ring the valley of Spielburg.
This is the forest that surrounds the castle.
"Go away.  This is just the barracks for us guards."
You are in the courtyard of Castle Spielburg.
The courtyard is paved with granite paving stones, carefully placed by a skilled craftsman.
There are two guards near the castle door.
He isn't here at the moment.
What you see, along with what you smell, gives the impression that the building on the east side of the courtyard contains the stables.
On the west side of the courtyard is a building that looks like soldier's barracks.
As a matter of fact, there's a soldier sleeping in front of it now.
The door to the Baron's castle is well guarded.
As you look back at the castle gate, you see a line of bushes along the south wall and the gatehouse and portcullis.
You barely get to sleep when you hear the guards searching the castle grounds for bums.  You decide it's time to make like a tree and leave.
There are too many guards around.
"Haven't you got anything better to do than hang around here?  Come back some other time you want to get rich."
The stable looks much better since you cleaned it.
It doesn't look like the stableman spends much of his time cleaning.
The castle's stable looks like it holds about six horses.  It could use some cleaning up.
The Baron was once known for his many fine and noble steeds.  Only a few aging animals remain in his stable.
The moon lights the Baron's courtyard.
You have raked the manure and covered the stable floor with new straw.
In the stable, the manure is beginning to pile up.
The castle is across the courtyard to the northeast.
The stable marks the eastern boundary of the Baron's courtyard.
Along the wall to the southeast is the gatehouse.  A line of bushes partially obscure a defensive pit along the wall.
Across the main courtyard to the west are the soldiers' barracks.
There is a fenced corral next to the stable building.
A low-looking lout.  HE could use some cleaning up.
He's not standing where you can see him.
"Don't tire me out with a bunch of questions.  Can't you see what a hard life I have?"
It's too early in the day; come back later.
It would be more comfortable to sleep on clean hay in the stable.
"Hey you!  It's bad enough you hang around here all day, but I sure ain't gonna let you sleep here before you've worked enough to earn it!"
"If you put in a day's work, and you wanna take a nap, it's OK with me."
"These horses, they act just like animals or somethin'."
"Just get out and wander around a little.  You'll find out."
"Don't ask me about anything to do with magic.  I don't wanna know."
"Sure!  C'mon in and grab a rake."
"Don't tire me out with a bunch of questions.  I don't need your comments, either.  Can't you see what a hard life I have?"
"Sorry, the Baron's horses aren't for sale."
The stableman hands you some coins and says,  "Now you're five silvers richer."
You curl up on some clean hay and get some rest.
It IS a rude awakening, however.
"WHAT DO YOU THINK THIS IS, A REST HOME???  GET TO WORK!  NOW!!!"
"Well, if you don't want to answer..."
"Good!  Come in here and take a rake."
Too bad!  I could use some help."
"That's what I'm asking YOU about, nimrod."
"I see you're back.  Need some work?"
"Quit fidgeting and answer me...yes or no!"
Getting into the castle the hard way.
So you says to yourself, "Self", you says...
You face the main doors of the Baron's keep.
The castle grounds look like no one has taken care of them.  The castle is still impressive close up, but it looks rather dirty
You see the castle and the guards
Along the wall to the southeast, you can see the stables.
You see the main courtyard of the castle.
Along the wall to the southwest, you can see the barracks.
The doors are securely locked for the night.
The doors are guarded by Frederick and Pierre.
The castle walls are very high.  You judge that you would not be able to climb them.
The doors are securely locked and barred from the inside.
You barely get to sleep when the guard on night patrol kicks you out.
Plays a mean horn.  A member in good standing of the musicians guild.
You don't think the guards would allow you to do so.

No sooner do you draw your weapon then the guards have their weapons out and quickly disarm you.  It seems that they practice with the Baron's Weapons Master.  They promptly drag you into the castle and down into the dungeon, which is not a very likely spot for the attainment of hero status.
The horns are tarnished and starting to corrode.
The guards look to be middle-aged, but in good physical condition.  Their clothing is faded and patched.  Even the horns look dull.
He's very proud of his moustache, but don't mention his bald spot.
The torch lights the way to the Baron's Hall.
You can't reach the windows from these trees.
Weeds grow profusely, but nobody notices.
This is the castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg.
These flagstones are from a rock quarry in eastern Germany.
Well...maybe the flagstones are from western Germany.
Granite from our very own mountains?
The Baron von Spielburg and Baronet await you in the great hall.
"No one enters the castle without the Baron's permission."
"This is the castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg."
"The Baron's daughter has been gone for years."
"The barracks are off to my right, but you have no business going there."
I told you not to mention it.  Now you've hurt his feelings, and he won't talk until he gets over it.
"Looks like meathead is sulking.  If I talk to you anymore he'll get even with me."
"Karl is the gatekeeper.  He is a good one to answer questions."
"You will have to see the Baron to get his permission."
The Baron is busy and will see no one.
The Baronet is in the castle recovering from his ordeal and will see no one.
"The Baronet Barnard von Spielburg is missing."
"The stable is off to my left."
"My name is Pierre.  I'm one of the Baron's personal guards."
"It is not our place to answer questions."
"Why thank you.  Your manners have improved."
After a wonderful meal with the Baron and his son, a peaceful night's sleep in a featherdown bed, and a filling breakfast in bed, you are ready to go adventuring once more.
Your figure remains still and silent.
"Please step where I can see you."
There is nobody to open the portcullis.
Leave the climbing to those who know how.
You're not in a good place to climb.
The guards won't allow you to do that.
The castle looms over the gatehouse and looks rather impressive.
The gatehouse is a massive structure with a portcullis closing off access to the castle.
The towers stand out above the castle walls.
The sky is clear except for a few clouds.
The grass seems to grow well here.
You see the road leading past the Healer's house.
"Come in.  Come in.  You have been expected.  Baron von Spielburg awaits you in the castle."
"Who's there? Come out and show yourself!"
Once more the guard raises the portcullis.
The guard, sensing something is awry, raises the portcullis.
You're not a quick learner.  Now you known why the grass grows so well around the portcullis.
That smarts.  Maybe you should move a little more quickly.
Had you been healthier, you probably could have survived that fall.  In your weakened condition, however, you succumbed to your injuries.
Take a break.  It's Mueller time.
The gatehouse is more difficult to climb than it first appeared.
Remember what happened to Humpty Dumpty.
After climbing down the wall you says to yourself, "Self", you says, "that wasn't too bad."
You can't see much of the gatekeeper over the battlements.
"As you can see, the gate is open."
"I will not open for a faceless voice. Come out where I can see you."
"As you can see, the gate is closed."
Don't keep everybody waiting with idle talk.
"Then go, before I close the portcullis."
"Sorry.  I wasn't listening.  Could you repeat youself?"
"That's me. I guard the portcullis day after day."
"The portcullis is the metal grate that bars the entrance to the castle yard.
"This is the Castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg, Lord of this Valley."
"My Lord is busy organizing his troops."
"My Lord sees no one since the loss of his son and daughter."
"Ten years ago the Baron's daughter, Elsa, was stolen away by magic.  Five years back, the Baronet Barnard von Spielburg went on a hunt from which he did not return."
"This was all clearly part of Baba Yaga's curse."
"The Baronet Barnard von Spielburg was a proud young man with the dark hair and eyes of his father, the Baron."

"He rode off one morning and his horse returned without him.  The horse had been raked by the claws of some large animal.  There is still a reward offered for the safe return of the Baronet, but I fear he is dead."
"Elsa von Spielburg was a lovely young child with braided brown hair and bright blue eyes, like her mother, the Baroness, used to have."
"A large winged creature flew over the castle walls, grabbed Elsa, and vanished before the guards could fire upon it.  We searched everywhere for her, but to no avail."
"Even the jester, Yorick, joined the search for her.  The Baron sent a troup of guards to storm Baba Yaga's hut, but I think their skulls sit atop her fence now."
"Elsa von Spielburg would be about eighteen now."

"The stablekeeper could use a strong young person to help clean out the stable.  It does not pay much, but it is good, honest work.  If you would like to take the job, just ask me to open the portcullis."
"Cleaning up after the horses is a good way to build up your muscles."
"The Baron ordered Baba Yaga to leave and she first cursed the graveyard.  When he then sent the Baronial Guards after her, only the captain returned to the castle."

"He spoke the words,
   'Hear me, oh mighty Baron.
You have angered me, and thus you are cursed.
All that you value you shall lose;
all that you treasure shall be taken from you.'
The captain then dissolved into a pool of sticky blackness."
"He was a very jolly fool who loved to laugh.  He was devoted to Elsa and swore he would not return until Elsa was safe."
"The powerful Ogress Baba Yaga is the cause of all the Baron's misfortune.  She is a vile creature who knows much magic."
"There is still a reward of 50 gold offered for the safe return of the Baronet and Elsa von Spielburg."
"For the capture or death of the brigand Leader there is the reward of 60 gold and the title of 'Hero of the Realm of Spielburg'."
"The brigands are protected by a magic user.  Not much is known about him, but he seems to use more magic potions and powders than spells."

"The leader of the brigands must be a good strategist.  He has organized and led many raids which result in much treasure and little loss on the part of the brigands.  Without their leader, the brigands would soon flee."
"There are now too few guards to take out the brigands, who still rob our people and drive away the traders.  They hide somewhere near the edge of this valley."
"This was once a very wealthy valley.  Many merchants have been robbed by the brigands.  There is a merchant in town who could tell you about his recent loss."
"Baba Yaga cursed the graveyard such that the dead can not rest at night.  One cannot go near the graveyard at dark without risking one's own death unless one uses protection."
"There is an unguent you can purchase from the Healer which protects against the dead that walk at night."
"'Hero of the Realm of Spielburg' is a title of respect and honor.  A true hero can release this land from its curse by using intelligence and courage."
"There are many foul creatures stalking the forest.  The Baron has too few guards to protect this valley from them."
"Spielburg Valley was once a prosperous and peaceful place before the Baron lost his family.  Now the Baron will not leave his castle and has no idea of the ruin this land has come to."
"Baba Yaga has her hut to the northwest.  It is a very dangerous place and many Baronial Guards were lost there."
"The Baroness died soon after the birth of her daughter.  The Baron still mourns for her."
"The Baron von Spielburg lost most of his guards trying to defeat Baba Yaga."
"The gatehouse is a shelter and resting place for the guards."
"Everybody talks about the weather, but nobody does anything about it."
"The Weapons Master often practices in the castle courtyard.  He gives lessons in the art of swordplay.  The lessons are expensive, but well worth the price."
"I wouldn't know about that, I don't get around much these days."

"It is a steep mountain to the northeast."
"That is the Spell that lets you cast a Magical Flame upon your enemy.  The Spell Scroll will cost you 60 silver."
